title: Pan-Seared Chestnuts
description: A simple and delicious side dish made with pan-seared chestnuts, perfect for the fall season.

ingredients.name: American
ingredients.notes: Note: American is not a typical ingredient, assuming this is a type of cheese or a brand name.

ingredients.name: midnight
ingredients.notes: Note: Midnight is not a typical ingredient, assuming this is a flavor or a type of salt.

ingredients.name: saute
ingredients.notes: Note: Saute is a cooking method, not an ingredient.

ingredients.name: skillet
ingredients.notes: Note: Skillet is cooking vessel, not an ingredient.

instructions: 1. Peel the chestnuts and place them in a large bowl.
2. Add enough water to the bowl to cover the chestnuts.
3. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the chestnuts are tender.
4. Drain the chestnuts and pat them dry with a paper towel.
5. Heat a skillet over medium-high heat.
6. Add a small amount of oil or butter to the skillet and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
7. Add the chestnuts to the skillet and sear them for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are nicely browned.
8. Remove the chestnuts from the skillet and season them with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Serve the chestnuts hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
